title: 502 Bad Gateway after updating n8n on Docker with Nginx
symptom: After updating n8n, accessing the UI results in a 502 Bad Gateway error. Restarting the container temporarily resolves the issue, but it returns.
applies_when: Using n8n with Docker and Nginx
Updated to a newer version of n8n
root_cause: The 502 Bad Gateway error typically arises from compatibility issues between the new n8n version and the existing Docker/Nginx setup, or due to insufficient resources allocated to the n8n container after the update.
fix_steps: Check the n8n container logs for specific error messages using 'docker logs n8n --tail 100 -f'.
Increase memory limits in the docker-compose.yml file to allocate more resources to the n8n container.
Configure the task runner settings in the docker-compose.yml if using n8n 2.x.
Update the Nginx configuration to support WebSockets and ensure proper proxy settings.
Test the Nginx configuration and reload it.
Check for port conflicts and ensure n8n is running and accessible.
agent_instruction: Follow the steps to check logs, increase memory, configure task runners, and update Nginx settings.
